evidence: Central compatibility facades are still acting as long-lived architectural hubs: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` mixes normalization/logging/toast presentation wiring and deprecated aliases, while `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exposes both explicit runtime bootstrap and a deprecated proxy export (`supabase`) with lazy fallback behavior.
`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` is a deprecated compatibility surface with 66 importers, indicating migration residue remains on a high-traffic boundary rather than at isolated adapters.

Wide public hubs with weak domain boundaries remain in core import paths: `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` (205 importers) and `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` (101 importers) aggregate cross-cutting exports, encouraging broad coupling through convenience entry points.

UI module ownership is blurred in key components: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` owns modal shell rendering plus Safari viewport repair logic, scroll locking, tasks pane offset policy, and event choreography; `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx` concentrates state-shape contracts, interaction policy, and rendering.
src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts constructs a memoized object that mostly mirrors input fields 1:1 with no validation/translation.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx and src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx re-spread the same button-group structures again before passing them to layout components.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts and src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts introduce multi-stage param-bag builders that mostly repack existing hook outputs into another facade type.

Shared edge-function seam exposes multiple boundary contracts at once: `parseJsonBody` returns `OperationResult`, `parseJsonBodyStrictResponse` returns `{ body, errorResponse }` legacy adapter, while billing trigger is fire-and-forget with console logging only (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts`, `supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts`).

Travel generation request boundary supports both v2 nested params and legacy flat fields in one normalizer with silent defaults, then force-casts to `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput`, weakening seam clarity (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts`).

Cancellation and lifecycle intent is not propagated consistently across integration seams: settings fetch accepts `AbortSignal` but downstream scope fetches do not consume it, while invalidation scheduling uses shared timeout stores that outlive individual callsites (`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts`).

Handler API shape is spread across multiple prop/type surfaces with near-duplicate signatures, increasing drift risk at module boundaries (`src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/types.ts`, `src/shared/types/imageHandlers.ts`).

Multiple compatibility shims are one-line pass-throughs with wildcard re-exports (`export *`) in `src/shared/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts:5`, `src/shared/lib/NetworkStatusManager.ts:5`, `src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts:5`, `src/shared/lib/autoplayMonitor.ts:5`, and `src/shared/lib/clientThumbnailGenerator.ts:5`, which broadens public surface implicitly and obscures what is intentionally kept during migration.

Migration markers are inconsistently enforced: some files include explicit owner/removal contracts (`src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts`) while others are open-ended deprecated shims without removal target metadata (`src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/useBatchImageLoading.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useClickRipple.ts`).

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:99-100` uses `void LEGACY_SUPABASE_PROXY_REMOVE_BY` and `void LEGACY_SUPABASE_PROXY_OWNER` sentinel references to silence unused values, which is ceremonial migration scaffolding instead of executable lifecycle enforcement.

`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:64` casts `deleteGenerationMutation.variables as string`, making delete-id state recovery dependent on unchecked runtime shape rather than typed mutation variables.

Task creators use catch -> handleError(..., showToast:false) -> throw error in multiple places (`createImageGenerationTask`, `createBatchImageGenerationTasks`, `createMagicEditTask`, `createJoinClipsTask`, `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask`), rethrowing the original unknown value instead of a normalized error contract.

Mutation/data hooks mix error contracts: `useGenerationMutations` throws wrapped `new Error(...)` in several paths but throws raw `variantError` in another path, while `useTasks` throws custom `new Error(...)` without shared normalization.

`useLoraSubmission` mixes direct user toasts + `return null` control flow in `uploadToHuggingFaceIfNeeded` with centralized `handleError` in outer catch, producing inconsistent propagation and observability for similar failures.

findings.summary: Deprecated compatibility facades remain primary hubs, blurring ownership and prolonging old architecture.
evidence: `handleError.ts` still exports deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) and also hosts presenter wiring (`setErrorPresenter`) alongside normalization/logging/toast orchestration.
`client.ts` provides both `initializeSupabase/getSupabaseClient` and deprecated `supabase` proxy export with fallback initialization path, keeping two access models active.
`useMobile.tsx` is explicitly deprecated but remains a compatibility export surface with high importer count in packet context.
findings.suggestion: Time-box the migration and enforce a single boundary per concern: remove deprecated aliases/proxy exports after codemod-based callsite migration, move UI-facing presentation wiring out of core error facade, and gate legacy entry points with CI checks that fail on new imports.

findings.summary: High-fan-in shared barrels flatten domain boundaries and create catch-all dependency entry points.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` is a canonical barrel with very high fan-in and re-exports mixed generation/shot type families, making dependency direction less explicit.
`utils.ts` is a thin shared barrel (`cn`, string formatting helpers) with high import fan-in, encouraging generic imports instead of domain-local modules.
`button.tsx` imports `cn` through `@/shared/lib/utils`, reinforcing reliance on broad utility hubs instead of narrow, purpose-specific module boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Replace broad barrels with narrower domain entry points (e.g., `types/generation/*`, `types/shot/*`, `shared/lib/ui/*`) and migrate hot paths to direct imports; reserve top-level barrels for package boundaries only, not internal convenience.

findings.summary: Several UI modules own multiple policy layers, reducing clarity of subsystem responsibilities.
evidence: `LightboxShell.tsx` combines shell rendering with platform-specific viewport repair, scroll-lock policy, pane-layout policy, and low-level pointer/touch orchestration.
`ShotActions.tsx` defines multiple state/callback contract interfaces and implements interaction decision policy (navigate vs add vs add-without-position) in the same render module.

`useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` centralizes diverse UI state concerns (star toggling, info tooltip, drag state, shot modal lifecycle), indicating unclear ownership boundaries across gallery item behaviors.
findings.suggestion: Split modules by stable responsibility boundaries: isolate platform/scroll policy into dedicated hooks, extract shot action decision logic into a pure policy module, and divide gallery item state into focused hooks (selection/info, shot-creation, star state) consumed by lean view components.

findings.summary: Lightbox button-group abstractions add wrapper layers but little behavioral value, increasing indirection cost.
evidence: useButtonGroupProps copies each field from input groups into a new object with no transformation logic.
Video/image render-model assembly re-wraps and re-spreads the same group fields before passing to layout.
The seam contract is broad and repetitive, forcing many files to keep synchronized prop-shape ceremony.
findings.suggestion: Remove useButtonGroupProps as a dedicated abstraction and define a single layout-facing prop contract; pass raw handler/state groups directly from shared state to layout, with only true boundary translation retained.

findings.summary: Task/orchestrator identifiers are written in multiple JSON shapes, forcing fragile multi-path lookup logic at completion boundaries.
evidence: payloadBuilder stores run_id and orchestrator_task_id top-level, while joinClips nests key orchestration identity under orchestrator_details.
orchestrator.ts must query params->>orchestrator_run_id, params->>run_id, and params->orchestrator_details->>run_id (and same pattern for orchestrator_task_id).
generation routing also reads parent_generation_id from multiple locations (top-level and orchestrator_details), confirming seam inconsistency.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one canonical orchestration identity schema (single location for run/orchestrator/parent IDs) with shared read/write helpers used by both client task builders and edge completion handlers; migrate legacy reads behind a temporary compatibility adapter.

findings.summary: Inline edit state assembly creates large transient param bags while downstream memoization is keyed to unstable object identity.
evidence: useInlineEditState builds ImageEditValue params by spreading multiple builder outputs into a new object each render.
useImageEditValue memoizes with dependency [params], so the memoized state recomputes whenever caller allocates a new params object.
The combined effect preserves complexity and object churn without meaningful memoization payoff.
findings.suggestion: Either remove useMemo in useImageEditValue and simplify to direct state construction, or stabilize dependencies by memoizing sub-slices in useInlineEditState and passing a referentially stable params object with fine-grained dependency keys.

findings.summary: Tooltip primitive and touch wrapper form a circular import that weakens module seam clarity and increases initialization risk.
evidence: tooltip.tsx re-exports TouchableTooltip from touchableTooltip.tsx.
touchableTooltip.tsx imports Tooltip/TooltipContent/TooltipProvider/TooltipTrigger from tooltip.tsx.
This creates a cycle between primitive and extension layers instead of a one-direction dependency.
findings.suggestion: Split base tooltip primitives into a cycle-free module (e.g., tooltipPrimitives.tsx), let both files import from that base, and keep re-exports in a separate index module that does not get imported by implementation files.

findings.summary: Shared edge helpers use mixed request/response contracts, making cross-function integration behavior hard to predict.
evidence: `parseJsonBody` returns `OperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>`, but `parseJsonBodyStrictResponse` returns a legacy `{ body?, errorResponse? }` adapter (`edgeRequest.ts:140-223`).
`triggerCostCalculation` swallows all failures and returns `Promise<void>` with only console logging (`billing.ts:101-131`), unlike the structured error envelope pattern used elsewhere.

`checkRateLimit` intentionally fails open on RPC/catch and returns `allowed: true` (`rateLimit.ts:78-120`), while `rateLimitResponse` still emits strict 429 envelopes, creating policy ambiguity at seam boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Standardize shared edge boundaries on a single contract (prefer `OperationResult` + explicit policy metadata), then refactor billing/rate-limit helpers to return typed outcomes instead of side-effect-only logging.

findings.summary: Video generation request assembly accepts two schemas simultaneously and silently fills defaults, obscuring boundary intent.
evidence: `TravelRequestBodyInput` includes both nested `motionParams/generationParams/seedParams` and legacy flat fields (`generateVideoService.ts:470-490`).
`normalizeTravelRequestBodyInput` auto-fills missing values (e.g., fallback phase config, default seed/motion mode) rather than validating required fields (`generateVideoService.ts:492-526`).
Final submission casts `requestBody` via `as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` (`generateVideoService.ts:761`), bypassing compile-time contract honesty.

Motion payload composition is split across `buildTravelRequestBodyV2` and shared `buildMotionTaskFields`, increasing seam indirection (`generateVideoService.ts:528-611`, `segmentSettingsUtils.ts:104-119`).
findings.suggestion: Deprecate flat legacy input path behind a separate adapter, enforce one canonical request schema with runtime validation, and remove unsafe cast at task creation boundary.

findings.summary: Abort/cancellation semantics are inconsistently propagated across async seams, leading to stale or non-cancelable work.
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` accepts an `AbortSignal`, but downstream `fetchAllScopes` performs Supabase calls without signal-aware cancellation (`toolSettingsService.ts:121-149`, `203-235`).
Single-flight dedupe returns existing promise by key regardless of caller signal (`toolSettingsService.ts:215-219`), so aborted callers can still inherit non-abortable in-flight work.

Invalidation uses module-level timeout maps for sync/variant paths (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:26-28`) and deferred execution (`52-88`, `90-119`), with no explicit global teardown for those shared stores.
findings.suggestion: Thread cancellation tokens through all async boundary helpers and make dedupe entries cancellation-aware; add explicit timeout-store lifecycle management for shared invalidation queues.

findings.summary: Shot image handler contracts are partially duplicated across modules, creating drift-prone integration seams.
evidence: Core handlers are centralized in `imageHandlers.ts`, but `ShotImageManager` props still define additional local callback shapes and optional variants across multiple grouped interfaces (`types.ts:26-143`).

`ShotBatchItemMobileProps.onDuplicate` uses an inline signature `(shotImageEntryId, timeline_frame)` instead of directly aliasing shared handler types (`types.ts:151`), increasing mismatch risk with `ImageDuplicateHandler` (`imageHandlers.ts:39`).
Two semantically close add-to-shot callbacks (`onAddToShot`, `onAddToShotWithoutPosition`) share the same handler type (`types.ts:65-67`), reducing boundary clarity for consumers.
findings.suggestion: Collapse component-local callback signatures onto shared aliases from `imageHandlers.ts`, and replace ambiguous parallel callbacks with a single typed operation mode parameter.

findings.summary: Wildcard compatibility re-exports make migration shims non-explicit and harder to retire safely.
evidence: Each file forwards via `export * from ...` with no explicit symbol list, so newly added upstream exports automatically leak through legacy paths.
This pattern increases hidden coupling and makes removal-gate auditing harder during migration cleanup.
findings.suggestion: Replace `export *` shims with explicit named re-exports of only required legacy symbols, and keep a minimal curated surface so migration diff and eventual deletion are deterministic.

findings.summary: Deprecated shim files use inconsistent sunset metadata, leaving migration debt tracking uneven.
evidence: Some deprecations include owner/removal target tags (`@deprecationOwner`, `@removalTarget`), while several shims only state `@deprecated` without enforceable removal metadata.
Mixed deprecation formats reduce local clarity on which compatibility layers are temporary vs effectively permanent.
findings.suggestion: Standardize a single deprecation header template across all shims (owner, removal date, and removal gate) and apply it to every migration facade file in this batch.

findings.summary: Delete confirmation hook uses an unchecked cast for mutation variables instead of typed mutation contracts.
evidence: `deletingId` is derived by `(deleteGenerationMutation.variables as string)`.
The hook already tracks `pendingDeleteId`, but runtime variable typing is bypassed with an assertion, which hides mismatches if mutation input shape changes.
findings.suggestion: Type `useDeleteGeneration` mutation variables explicitly as `string` and remove the cast; alternatively store `deletingId` from local state transitions instead of reading asserted mutation internals.

findings.summary: Shared-layer tests follow conflicting directory conventions, weakening predictable ownership boundaries.
evidence: Hook tests are grouped under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/...`.
Component tests in the same `src/shared` layer are colocated as `*.test.tsx` beside source files instead of using a matching `__tests__` structure.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one test-location contract for `src/shared` (either colocated or `__tests__` folders) and migrate these files to that single convention to make package boundaries and test ownership predictable.

findings.summary: Neighboring modules reference divergent shared-lib namespaces, indicating incomplete package reorganization.
evidence: `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` imports `@/shared/lib/deepEqual`, but its test mocks `@/shared/lib/utils/deepEqual`.

`useApiTokens.ts` uses segmented namespaces (`@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, `@/shared/lib/queryKeys/api`), while `useAutoTopup.test.ts` mocks flatter legacy paths (`@/shared/lib/errorHandler`, `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`).
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce a single canonical shared-lib package map (including `deepEqual`, error handling, and query key modules), then update imports/mocks together so package boundaries reflect one architecture instead of parallel legacy/current paths.

findings.summary: Deprecation governance is strict for one shim but informal for other public contracts.
evidence: README.md defines an explicit enforcement command for `@/types/shots` removal (`npm run check:shots-shim-usage`).

`client.ts` and `handleError.ts` include deprecation metadata and removal dates (`@removalTarget 2026-06-30`) but no corresponding automated gate; `client.ts` even `void`s governance constants to keep them compile-visible only.
findings.suggestion: Add a shared deprecation policy gate in `quality:check` that fails on expired `@removalTarget` markers and legacy import usage (including `supabase` compatibility proxy and `reportRuntimeError` aliases), so all contract sunsets are enforced consistently.

findings.summary: Supabase bootstrap contract is documented as explicit but runtime still relies on hidden lazy recovery.
evidence: README.md says bootstrap calls `initializeSupabase()` once and frames this as the runtime contract.
`getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime-access errors and initializes lazily as fallback; warning is DEV-only and production path silently tolerates pre-bootstrap access.
findings.suggestion: Time-box the fallback: keep it behind an explicit migration flag, emit telemetry in all environments, and switch to fail-fast after the sunset date so runtime behavior matches the documented contract.

findings.summary: A generic shared utils barrel obscures UI utility ownership and weakens boundary clarity.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils`.
`utils.ts` is a tiny barrel that re-exports unrelated helpers (`cn`, `cropFilename`, `truncateText`) under a generic name, encouraging cross-domain catch-all imports.
findings.suggestion: Replace broad `@/shared/lib/utils` imports with ownership-specific paths (for example `@/shared/lib/uiClassNames` for `cn`) and retire or narrow the generic barrel to prevent future boundary erosion.

findings.summary: Multiple task flows log via handleError but rethrow raw errors, bypassing a single propagated error shape
evidence: Each module catches `unknown`, calls `handleError(...)`, then uses `throw error` (original value) instead of `throw handleError(...)`/`handleAndRethrow(...)`.
`handleAndRethrow` exists in shared error utilities but is not used in these high-traffic task creation boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Standardize boundary behavior to `handleAndRethrow(error, options)` (or `throw handleError(...)`) in these catch blocks so upstream callers always receive normalized `AppError` with preserved context.

findings.summary: Generation mutation helpers throw inconsistent error types and bypass shared normalization in helper paths
evidence: `useGenerationMutations` throws `new Error(...)` in several helper failures, but for variant creation it logs with `console.error` and throws raw `variantError` directly.
`useTasks` query functions throw ad-hoc `new Error(...)` without shared normalization, while nearby mutation hooks rely on `handleError` in `onError`.
findings.suggestion: Normalize helper-level failures before throw (wrap Supabase errors consistently and avoid raw error throws), and replace ad-hoc console-only paths with shared normalization so all task/generation data hooks expose one predictable error contract.

findings.summary: LoRA submission flow mixes toast-only failure handling with silent null returns instead of propagating typed errors
evidence: `uploadToHuggingFaceIfNeeded` handles upload failure with `toast.error(...)` and returns `null`, and caller exits early after `setIsSubmitting(false)` with no shared error record.
The same hook also has an outer `catch` using `handleError(...)`, creating two different failure pipelines for closely related submit/upload failures.
findings.suggestion: Refactor upload helpers to throw a typed/domain error and let one top-level catch perform `handleError` + user notification; avoid `null` sentinel returns for failure paths.
